calendar.matters:
Petition For Instructions Order To Reimburse Attorney Expenses And To Accept Verification From Distributees Under Japanese Law
Off Calendar Jul-18-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Rosenberg-Wohl, via Zoom, for and with Kim Ridinger, Administrator.

The court and counsel discuss the petition for final distribution which was taken under submission on May-19-2021 with the court reminding counsel that if he is seeking reimbursement of expenses that he failed to request prior to the court taking the petition for final distribution under submission on the date referenced above he should have sought relief within six (6) months via CCP 473(b). Counsel offers testimony in support of his petition. The court points counsel to probate code 11605 and advises counsel that since the Japanese beneficiaries have signed receipts under penalty of perjury under California law, that matter is now moot. The court and counsel discuss the issue with his reimbursement request and the cash on hand and that two beneficiaries have received less than the amounts they should have received as ordered in the order for final distribution.

After additional discussion, the matter is taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Cecilia Castellanos. Examiner: Sandra Hilton. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.

Petition For Instructions Order To Reimburse Attorney Expenses And To Accept Verification From Distributees Under Japanese Law

Petition For Instructions Order To Reimburse Attorney Expenses And To Accept Verification From Distributees Under Japanese Law
Off Calendar May-31-2022 Continued To Jun-21-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar May-26-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Rosenberg-Wohl appeared by zoom for and with Kim Ridinger (Administrator) by zoom.

The Court noted that an estate for Fumiko has been opened. Mr. Rosenberg-Wohl explained services provided and time spent to support his request for extraordinary fees related to the Japanese legal issues, for his fee request related to time spent drafting the Decedent's will, spoke of time spent by himself and his Client while operating under the assumption that there was no will, and spoke of the time it took to get the safe deposit box opened. Mr. Ridinger addressed the issue of the funds being held in a non-interest-bearing account and not being fully FDIC insured stating that his thought was that this wasn't going to be very long, that there was no money for the first two years, and that the matter kept being continued and continued. Mr. Ridinger also spoke of the interest only being about $12.00 a month, the difficulty he had with Merrill Lynch not being able to open a brokerage account, the numerous trips he had to take to Bank of America to get the account opened, acknowledged that the FDIC insurance does not insure the entire amount of the funds in the account and stated that he would have had to go to several banks. The Third Amended Corrected and Supplemented First and Final Account and Report of Administrator, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Sep-23-2020 Continued To Nov-18-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Rosenberg-Wohl appeared by zoom for and with Kim Ridinger (Administrator) by zoom.

The Court stated that it received the additional information, that it's been reviewed and provided Mr. Rosenberg-Wohl with its tentative ruling. Discussion was held regarding there being no evidence to show that Toshiro and Sachiko are the personal representatives of Fumiko's estate, the role of a personal representative and someone being an heir, this Court not having discretion under Probate Codes 11802(a) to distribute directly to the Heirs of a Beneficiary, etc. Mr. Rosenberg-Wohl stated that they have had discussion about the backup possibility of opening probate for Fumiko here, that the concern is that Fumiko had never been here and didn't reside here, and that he has to figure out how to best proceed. The Court advised Mr. Rosenberg-Wohl that the issue of direct distribution is off the table, and provided Mr. Rosenberg-Wohl with the option of continuing the matter for him to figure out how to open an estate for Fumiko, or taking the matter off calendar. Mr. Rosenberg asked to have the matter continued. The Second Amended Corrected and Supplemented First and Final Account, etc. is continued to November 18, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Aug-25-2020 Continued To Sep-23-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: David Rosenberg-Wohl, Esq., counsel for and with Kim Ridinger, Administrator (will annexed) (appearing separately by video conferencing).

At the first calendar call, Mr. Ridinger is present but Mr. Rosenberg-Wohl is not. The Court directs Mr. Ridinger to contact counsel and passes the matter.

At the second call, Mr. Rosenberg-Wohl and Mr. Ridinger are present. The Court notes the purpose of the day's hearing then queries counsel as to the status of the ostensible Japanese personal representatives. Mr. Rosenberg-Wohl advises the Court of the filing of a supplemental declaration on the issue which the Court acknowledges but finds unpersuasive. The Court requests additional and hopefully more detailed and substantive explication on the requirements for becoming a personal representative - or similar status in Japan, noting that the status of a "personal representative" is normally determined by state authority, e.g., a court proceeding. Counsel should address this issue in the context of the Japanese legal system.

Moving on, the Court notes that certain "creditor claims" by Mr. Ridinger read as statutory fees, rather than extraordinary fees. Mr. Rosenberg-Wohl will submit on the matter. The Court also queries Mr. Rosenberg-Wohl as to his fee agreement which Mr. Rosenberg-Wohl confirms there is no fee agreement but rather, an oral understanding with Japanese counsel. The issue of the amount of the bond will need to be addressed as well.

The Court continues the matter to September 23, 2020 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. Supplemental pleadings must be filed and courtesy copies delivered to the Court Examiner on or before September 15, 2020 by 12:00 p.m.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: Not Reported. (D204)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Sep-25-2019 Continued To Oct-23-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Rosenberg-Wahl for and with Kim Ridinger.

The Court noted that this is not ready for final distribution, that they don't have any schedules, that there is a request for distribution to the Decedent's Mother, but she is deceased, that this has not been properly noticed, that an accounting has not been filed and that this is not in a condition to go forward. Mr. Rosenberg-Wahl stated that he is at a loss and that it was his understanding that everything had been filed. The Court instructed Mr. Rosenberg-Wahl to be sure to provide courtesy copies of all documents to the Court and questions whether this should go off calendar. Mr. Rosenberg-Wahl stated that he is not sure how to answer that. The First and Final Report of Administrator, etc. is continued to October 23, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Counsel to correct all of the issues and to be sure everything is filed by October 16, 2019.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Wohl for Kim Ridinger.

Mr. Wohl requested a one month continuance stating that he is nearly ready, and explained that the inventory and appraisal has not been filed because it has taken his Client some time to get a hold of the requisite financial documents. Status on close of estate is continued to July 9, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Wohl is to file the inventory and appraisal, and either a petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of report on status of administration no later than July 3, 2019.
